---
title: "Mixture of Distillations"
description: ""
date: "2026-03-05"
categories: [rl]
status: "exploring"
---

See slide deck [here](./mixture_distillations_slides.pdf). 

## Methods comparison 

| **Method** | **Training** | **Merging** | **Forgetting fix** |
|---|---|---|---|
| Sequential RLVR (baseline) | Sequential | N/A | None (the problem) |
| GLM-5 cross-stage distillation | Sequential | Sequential (recovery stage) | Retroactive repair |
| Multi-objective RLVR | **Simultaneous** | N/A (one model) | Avoided by design |
| Feedback prediction rehearsal | Sequential | N/A (one model) | Concurrent maintenance loss |
| Teacher-based regularization | Sequential | N/A (one model) | Concurrent regularization |
| Population RLVR | **Parallel** | One-shot merge | Avoided by design |
| Mixture of Distillations | **Parallel** | Routed distillation | Avoided by design |

The parallel methods (population, MoD) never forget because specialists never train on each other's tasks. The simultaneous method (multi-objective) never forgets because all tasks are optimized together. The sequential methods (rehearsal, teacher reg) accept sequential training but add a maintenance signal to slow forgetting. GLM-5 accepts forgetting and repairs it after the fact.
